data_models.name: ErrorResponse
data_models.description: Canonical error response shape for all backend errors.

data_models.name: ErrorObject
data_models.description: Error details object nested in ErrorResponse.error.

data_models.name: Task
data_models.description: Android local task model for task list, estimates, and completion tracking.

fields.name: completed_pomodoros
fields.description: Completed pomodoros attributed to this task (>= 0).

data_models.name: Session
data_models.description: Android local record of a completed timer segment (work/break/long_break).

fields.name: duration_ms
fields.description: Computed duration in milliseconds; must equal end_epoch_ms - start_epoch_ms.

fields.name: linked_task_id
fields.description: Optional Task.id linked when starting a work segment.

fields.name: plan_label
fields.description: Optional label (e.g., 'classic' or custom plan name) for analytics/export.

data_models.name: TimerPlan
data_models.description: Android local timer configuration for classic defaults or advanced custom sequences.

fields.name: classic_work_minutes
fields.description: Classic work duration minutes (default 25). Required when mode='classic'.

fields.name: classic_break_minutes
fields.description: Classic short break minutes (default 5). Required when mode='classic'.

fields.name: classic_long_break_minutes
fields.description: Classic long break minutes (default 15). Required when mode='classic'.

fields.name: classic_long_break_every_work_sessions
fields.description: Long break cadence (default 4). Required when mode='classic'.

fields.name: segments
fields.description: Custom sequence segments in order. Required when mode='custom_sequence'.

data_models.name: TimerSegment
data_models.description: One segment in a custom sequence.

data_models.name: AppSettings
data_models.description: Android local settings persisted in DataStore (not Room).

fields.name: sound_enabled
fields.description: Whether timer transition notifications may use sound (if not in DND-respect mode).

fields.name: vibration_enabled
fields.description: Whether timer transition notifications may vibrate (if not in DND-respect mode).

fields.name: silent_notifications
fields.description: If true, notifications must not use sound or vibration regardless of other toggles.

fields.name: respect_dnd
fields.description: If true, notifications must avoid sound/vibration when device is in DND (implementation must be DND-friendly).

fields.name: max_total_paused_minutes
fields.description: Maximum total paused minutes allowed per running segment (>= 0). When exceeded, timer must stop or force resume per TimerEngine rules.

fields.name: segment_end_behavior
fields.description: One of: 'auto_advance', 'prompt', 'ask_each_time'. 'ask_each_time' must prompt user at each segment end.

database.notes: Android: Room (SQLite) with schema versioning and at least one migration path (v1->v2). Backend: local SQLite for demo only; no auth. Backend DB is optional for current functionality but must be runnable locally.
database.ddl:
/* Android Room (SQLite) canonical tables (mirrors Entities)
Schema version v2.

-- v1
CREATE TABLE tasks (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  notes TEXT,
  estimate_pomodoros INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  completed_pomodoros INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  is_completed INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
  created_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  updated_at_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_tasks_is_completed ON tasks(is_completed);

-- v1 sessions without linked_task_id; v2 adds linked_task_id and index
CREATE TABLE sessions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('work','break','long_break')),
  start_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  end_epoch_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  duration_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  linked_task_id TEXT NULL,
  plan_label TEXT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (linked_task_id) REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE SET NULL
);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_start_epoch_ms ON sessions(start_epoch_ms);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_type ON sessions(type);
CREATE INDEX idx_sessions_linked_task_id ON sessions(linked_task_id);

-- Timer plans and custom segments
CREATE TABLE timer_plans (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  mode TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (mode IN ('classic','custom_sequence')),
  classic_work_minutes INTEGER,
  classic_break_minutes INTEGER,
  classic_long_break_minutes INTEGER,
  classic_long_break_every_work_sessions INTEGER
);
CREATE TABLE timer_plan_segments (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY NOT NULL,
  plan_id TEXT NOT NULL,
  position INTEGER NOT NULL,
  type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (type IN ('work','break','long_break')),
  duration_minutes INTEGER NOT NULL,
  FOREIGN KEY (plan_id) REFERENCES timer_plans(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_timer_plan_segments_plan_pos ON timer_plan_segments(plan_id, position);

-- Migration requirement
-- v1->v2: ALTER TABLE sessions ADD COLUMN linked_task_id TEXT NULL; plus index idx_sessions_linked_task_id.
*/

invariants: All clients use the same endpoint paths and JSON fields as defined here.
No renaming of routes, fields, or tables without updating contract.
Backend must return JSON errors in a consistent shape.
Session strategy is 'none': no login endpoints, no cookies, and no JWTs; all endpoints must have auth_required=false.
GET /health must always return HTTP 200 and body exactly {"status":"ok"} when service is healthy.
Error responses must be JSON: {"error":{"code":string,"message":string,"details"?:object}}; no HTML error pages.
Android timer segment types are strictly one of: 'work', 'break', 'long_break' across DB, export CSV, and UI.
A Session record is persisted only for completed segments; duration_ms must equal end_epoch_ms - start_epoch_ms and must be > 0.
Streak day counts require >= 1 completed 'work' session per local calendar day; breaks do not count toward streak.
Classic mode default durations are 25/5/15 with long break after 4 completed work sessions unless user overrides via TimerPlan/AppSettings.
Pause cap: total paused time per active segment must not exceed AppSettings.max_total_paused_minutes; enforcement must be in the pure TimerEngine to keep rules testable.
Offline-first: Android app must not require backend availability for core features (timer, tasks, history, settings, export).
Room schema version must be incremented on any schema change and must include at least one tested migration path (v1->v2).
CSV export must include one row per completed Session with columns: id,type,start_epoch_ms,end_epoch_ms,duration_ms,linked_task_id,plan_label.
